title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) at home with our easy recipe. Learn to make tender, flavorful short ribs with the perfect marinade. Get cooking!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean BBQ Short Ribs
authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs recipe
best homemade Korean BBQ Short Ribs
seo.h1: Authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?
Ingredients for Korean BBQ Short Ribs
How to Make Galbi: Step-by-Step Instructions
Pro Tips for Tender & Flavorful Galbi
Serving Suggestions for Korean BBQ Short Ribs
seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) garnished with sesame seeds and green onions, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, savory, and slightly sweet flavors of homemade Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi), perfect for your next dinner!

description: Experience the iconic flavors of Korean BBQ with these tender, marinated short ribs. Galbi features a sweet and savory sauce that caramelizes beautifully on the grill, offering an unforgettable dining experience. Perfect for gatherings, these succulent ribs are a true taste of Korea.

ingredients.notes: grated or pureed (or 1/4 cup apple juice)

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the short ribs thoroughly under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them completely dry with paper towels. Score the meat lightly against the grain in a crosshatch pattern if desired, to help absorb the marinade.

instructions.title: Make the Galbi Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the soy sauce, brown sugar, grated Asian pear, minced garlic, grated ginger, grated onion, sesame oil, black pepper, and water. Whisk well until the sugar is fully dissolved and the marinade is smooth.

instructions.title: Marinate the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Add the prepared short ribs to the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. You may need to do this in batches or use a large ziploc bag. Cover the bowl or seal the bag and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight for best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare for Grilling
instructions.text: Remove the short ribs from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before cooking to bring them closer to room temperature. Preheat your grill (gas or charcoal) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Galbi
instructions.text: Place the marinated short ribs on the hot grill. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness and desired doneness, flipping occasionally. The ribs should have beautiful char marks and be cooked through but still tender. Alternatively, you can cook them in a hot cast-iron skillet.

instructions.title: Rest, Garnish, and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the Galbi to a cutting board and let them rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness. Slice the meat off the bone if desired, then garnish with toasted sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Serve hot with rice and your favorite banchan (Korean side dishes).

tips: For extra tender ribs, add 1-2 tablespoons of kiwi puree to the marinade. Kiwi contains enzymes that naturally tenderize meat, but dont marinate for more than 4-6 hours with kiwi or the meat can become mushy.
If you cant find Asian pear, a regular sweet apple (like Fuji or Gala) or 1/4 cup of apple juice can be used as a substitute for the sweetness and tenderizing properties.
Galbi is best served fresh off the grill. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheated gently, but the texture may change slightly.
article: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs is a beloved Korean Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
